title: Fruit Salad
instructions: In a large bowl, combine all fruits.
Serve immediately.

title: Brownies
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour an 8-inch square pan.
In a large bowl, mix together the butter, sugar, and eggs. Stir in vanilla.
In a separate bowl, combine cocoa, flour, salt, and baking powder. Gradually add to the egg mixture until well blended.
Spread the batter into the prepared pan.
Bake for 25 to 30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out with moist crumbs.
Cool in pan before cutting into squares.

title: Yogurt Parfait
instructions: In a glass or jar, layer yogurt, granola, and berries. Repeat the layers until the glass is full.
Serve immediately.

title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
In a large bowl, cream together the butter, white sugar, and brown sugar until smooth.
Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the vanilla.
In a separate bowl, combine the flour, baking soda, and salt. Gradually add to the creamed mixture.
Stir in the chocolate chips.
Drop by rounded tablespoons onto ungreased baking sheets.
Bake for 9 to 11 minutes, or until golden brown.
Cool on baking sheets for a few minutes before transferring to wire racks to cool completely.

title: Apple Crumble
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Place apples in a baking dish.
In a small bowl, combine flour, brown sugar, and oats. Cut in butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Sprinkle over the apples.
Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the apples are tender.

title: Cheesecake
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a small bowl, combine graham cracker crumbs and melted butter. Press into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan.
In a large bowl, beat cream cheese, sugar, and vanilla until smooth. Beat in eggs one at a time.
Pour the filling into the crust.
Bake for 50-60 minutes, or until the center is almost set.
Let cool completely before refrigerating for at least 4 hours.

title: Misti Doi
ingredients: 1 cup sugar or date palm jaggery (nolen gur)
instructions: Hang the starter yogurt in a muslin cloth for an hour to drain excess water.
In a heavy-bottomed pan, boil the milk until it reduces to about half its original volume.
Caramelize the sugar in a separate pan until it turns a deep amber color. Carefully add this to the reduced milk and stir well. If using jaggery, melt it and mix it in.
Let the milk cool down until it is lukewarm.
Whisk the hung yogurt starter until smooth, then mix it thoroughly into the lukewarm milk.
Pour the mixture into an earthen pot (or any setting dish).
Cover and keep in a warm, dark place for 8-12 hours to set.
Once set, refrigerate for at least 4 hours before serving.

title: Sandesh
instructions: Bring the milk to a boil. Add lemon juice to curdle it. Once the whey separates, strain the curdled milk solids (chhena) through a muslin cloth.
Rinse the chhena under cold water and hang it for 30 minutes to drain excess water.
Knead the chhena on a flat surface for 8-10 minutes until it becomes smooth and non-greasy.
Add powdered sugar and cardamom powder. Knead for another 2 minutes.
Gently heat the mixture in a non-stick pan on low heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring continuously, until it comes together.
Let the mixture cool slightly. Shape into small balls or use molds.
Garnish with pistachios or saffron.
